items.title: Remko Pasveer legt uit waarom hij terugkeert bij Heracles
items.summary: Een tikje ouder en in een totaal ander decor dan twaalf jaar eerder stond Remko Pasveer vrijdag op het veld van het Asito Stadion. De 42-jarige keeper keerde deze week terug bij Heracles Almelo en staat zondag direct in de basis tegen Fortuna Sittard. Hij legt uit waarom hij terug is. 'Ondanks de twijfel dacht ik uiteindelijk wel: ik ga het doen.'

items.title: Champions League playoff picks: Can Mourinho shock...
items.summary: The Champions League playoff draw has given us an instant rematch between José Mourinho's Benfica and Real Madrid. Here are our picks to advance.

items.title: Grading all 36 teams, plus our best XI of league p...

items.summary: ⚽ News and previews heading into the weekend’s action ⚽ Premier League: 10 things to look out for | Mail Niall Mikel Arteta and Eddie Howe will be speaking soon. Two titans of management speak. This was interesting from Slot. He was asked why Premier League teams are becoming so dominant in Europe, with Liverpool particularly impressive in the Champions League this season especially compared to many of their domestic showings. See also: Spurs. Continue reading...

items.title: PSV heeft Boadu in topper tegen Feyenoord mogelijk al terug
items.summary: Myron Boadu (25) keert mogelijk komend weekend al terug bij PSV. De spits stond maanden aan de kant door een blessure, maar kan in de topper tegen Feyenoord zijn rentree maken. Mits alles goed verloopt.

items.title: Madrid handed Benfica, Jose rematch in UCL draw
items.summary: Real Madrid have been handed a rematch with Jose Mourinho's Benfica in the Champions League knockout playoff round.

items.title: Carrick warns Utd: Don't underestimate Fulham
items.summary: Michael Carrick has said that if Manchester United underestimate Fulham after impressive wins over Manchester City and Arsenal it will be "bang out of order."

items.title: Barça's Flick on Dro: 'I really loved this boy'
items.summary: Barcelona coach Hansi Flick has revealed he was "disappointed" by Dro Fernández's decision to join Paris Saint-Germain this week, saying he "loved this boy."

items.title: Bosz leeft mee met Van Persie: 'Verschrikkelijk, ik vrees het ergste'
items.summary: Peter Bosz leefde donderdagavond mee met Robin van Persie. De trainer van PSV zag de hartverscheurende beelden van Feyenoord-spits Shaqueel van Persie bij Real Betis, die geblesseerd raakte . Dat emotioneerde de collega-trainer én voetbalvader.

items.title: Transfers PSV: Bosz sprak met Nagalo en roemt overbodige verdediger
items.summary: VI volgt het transfernieuws bij PSV op de voet, zeker nu de winterse transferwindow in aantocht is. De doelwitten van de Eindhovenaren, duiding van de clubwatcher, of aandacht in buitenlandse media. We verzamelen alles hier.

items.title: Prepare for a slugfest! Sabalenka and Rybakina meet in Australian Open final
items.summary: Aryna Sabalenka insists she has learned from last year's final heartache to Madison Keys as she prepares to land the Australian Open title for a third time.

items.title: Does Raducanu need to stick with same coach after latest change?
items.summary: Emma Raducanu is searching for a 10th coach in the last five years after splitting with Francis Roig following her early Australian Open exit.

items.title: Go Ahead wil na Sampsted ook rechtsback van PSV
items.summary: Go Ahead Eagles is met PSV in gesprek over een transfer van Eus Waayers. De Deventenaren hebben de negentienjarige rechtsback op het oog als stand-in van Alfons Sampsted. De IJslander werd vorige week binnengehaald als opvolger van Mats Deijl.

Lecky Watson fancied to outrun is odds in Irish Gold Cup
items.summary: Declan Rix and Sam Boswell join host Kate Tracey ahead of the Dublin Racing Festival, which takes place at Leopardstown this weekend.

items.title: Fulham sign Man City winger Bobb for £27m
items.summary: Fulham sign Norway winger Oscar Bobb from Manchester City for £27m on a five-and-a-half-year contract.

items.title: Celtic to play Stuttgart & Forest face Fenerbahce
items.summary: Celtic will play Stuttgart and Nottingham Forest will face Fenerbahce in two-legged play-offs to reach the last 16 of the Europa League.

items.title: Arteta compares Arsenal’s Max Dowman with young Messi as teenager commits future
items.summary: 16-year-old signs pre-agreement for first pro contract ‘What Max has done age 15, I haven’t seen before’ Mikel Arteta has likened Max Dowman to a young Lionel Messi after the teenager committed his future to Arsenal. Dowman, who in November became the youngest player to play in the Champions League at 15 years and 308 days, has signed a pre-agreement for his first professional contract when he turns 17 in December. It is understood several clubs were interested in the England Under-19s forward but there was never real doubt over whether the player who joined Arsenal aged six would stay. Continue reading...

items.title: Forest to face Fenerbahce as Celtic draw Stuttgart in Europa League play-offs
items.summary: Nottingham Forest will face Turkish giants Fenerbahce in the Europa League knockout play-off round, as Celtic were drawn against German side Stuttgart.

items.title: The Arsenal fan psychodrama: Big Defeat Headloss hits hard after United setback | Chris Godfrey
items.summary: I played out a torturous, all-too-familar dance after the Gunners’ title-race stumble. But if we’re suffering like this in January, how will we feel in May? I sometimes joke that I’m not sure I actually like football, just Arsenal. Hate-watching rivals aside, if a game doesn’t concern the Gunners it probably doesn’t concern me, such is my one-club tunnel vision. Even then, there are occasions where my love of Arsenal appears debatable. As a friend recently put it to me: “I’ve watched Arsenal games with you. I’m not sure you like Arsenal and yet you’re possibly the most fervent Gooner I know.” Ah, the torturous dance between joy and torment. I relived it again last Sunday evening, when Arsenal lost to Manchester United. On paper, it should have been simple enough to compartmentalise: you can’t win them all and we’re still four points clear at the top of the league table and looking strong in all three cups. And yet, for the first time this season, I succumbed to true result-induced head loss. Continue reading...
